page: Amazon Web Services m9g.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the AWS ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 CPU running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Cache specifications consist of 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 96 MB L3 cache. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and memory throughput. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 28529.16 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 329.97 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, with Redis handling 522.08 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and small-model LLM prompt processing falling into the average tier. Additionally, LLM text generation for a small 135M model is relatively slow, registering in the bottom tier at 22.59 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal architecture offers high resource density and dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading overhead, making it a viable option for workloads requiring predictable, scale-out compute resources. The lack of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, which may require external network-attached storage. Given its high multi-core throughput and memory bandwidth, this server is suited for multi-threaded applications, large-scale database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive containerized deployments, whereas single-threaded tasks or heavy LLM text generation may not fully leverage its hardware profile.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.metal-48xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 100 Gbps network interface, this server lacks local storage and GPUs. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database and Redis operations. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are low. This server is suited for highly parallelized workloads, large-scale database systems, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.metal-48xl is an arm64 bare-metal server with 192 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps networking, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.

768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 4.0 GB per core.
